html: Our paper Implanting Atomically Dispersed Fe and Mo Sites into 3D Scaffolds: A Scalable Route to Bifunctional Water Splitting Electrocatalysts is published in Small. Ion implantation anchors atomically dispersed Fe and Mo single atoms across 3D CNT/nickel-foam scaffolds at up to 8-inch scale; the bifunctional electrodes drive an anion-exchange-membrane electrolyzer at 500 mA cm⁻² and 1.66 V with over 100 h of stable operation.

Our paper Subsurface Vacancy Engineering Enables Atomically Clean and Oxidation-Resistant Copper Interfaces for Anode-Free Lithium Metal Batteries is accepted in ACS Nano. Ion implantation strips the native oxide from copper foil and seeds subsurface vacancies that keep the interface atomically clean and oxidation-resistant, reaching 98.8% Coulombic efficiency over 600 cycles in anode-free cells.

Our review Artificial Intelligence for Materials Science: Transforming Research Paradigms is out in Chemical Reviews. It surveys how AI is reshaping each stage of materials research, from design and synthesis through characterization to mechanism discovery.

Transforming the synthesis of carbon nanotubes with machine learning models and automation published in Matter. We pair machine-learning models with an automated growth platform to steer carbon-nanotube synthesis, turning manual trial-and-error into a data-driven loop. (ESI Highly Cited Paper)

Nano-seeding catalysts for wafer-scale horizontally aligned carbon nanotube arrays published in Nature Communications. A nano-seeding catalyst design grows high-density, horizontally aligned carbon-nanotube arrays that stay uniform across a full wafer. (Editors' Highlights)
